headline: The mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, says only people who live in ‘shitty houses’ oppose data center
summary: Shelbyville, Indiana mayor sparks controversy over a $2 billion data center. Mayor Scott Furgeson was caught on camera insulting opponents of the project. He suggested only those in "shitty houses" oppose the data center.

headline: Benn Jordan longs for the days of tech that didn’t spy on you
summary: Musician Benn Jordan has shifted his YouTube content to cybersecurity and surveillance. He expresses a desire for older tech that wasn't designed to spy on users. Jordan's channel now focuses on privacy concerns and digital ethics.

headline: 82-0 is the best basketball game, to hell with NBA 2K
summary: "82-0" is a new game combining fantasy basketball stats with instant gratification. Players draft a team aiming for a theoretical perfect 82-0 season. The game offers a unique twist on basketball simulation, distinct from NBA 2K.

headline: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
summary: Meta's standalone AI app now features a "For You" section. This section generates clickbait-style stories using AI. Topics, images, and text are AI-generated and often questionable.

headline: 007 First Light Dev IO Interactive Says It's Now Sold 3 Million Copies, Tracking 'Well Above Our Forecasts at This Point'
summary: IO Interactive's "007 First Light" has sold 3 million copies. The game achieved this milestone in under two weeks. Sales are "well above forecasts," according to the developer.

headline: Netflix Movie Boss Says 'We Won Work With' Filmmakers Who 'Still Want Theatrical' Releases
summary: Netflix film boss Dan Lin states the streamer won't prioritize theatrical releases. Lin says Netflix "won't work with" filmmakers insisting on big-screen premieres. This confirms Netflix's continued focus on its streaming-first model.

headline: Why The Last Ronin Is Perfect for a TMNT Game
summary: The new Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles game draws from "The Last Ronin" comic series. The article argues this source material is an ideal choice for a TMNT game. It highlights the potential for a unique and compelling TMNT video game experience.

headline: Masters of the Universe Proves Skeletor Will Always Be the Franchise’s MVP
summary: The new Masters of the Universe movie excels in its portrayal of Skeletor. Skeletor balances humor and threat, making him the film's funniest character. The article crowns Skeletor the franchise's MVP due to his compelling depiction.

headline: West Ham joint chairman David Sullivan steps down after 'serious' historical allegations
summary: David Sullivan resigns as West Ham joint chairman and director. His resignation follows "serious historic allegations" coming to light. The club confirms his immediate departure from the roles.

headline: The Nowak murder has lit a match under British politics. This is how we got here
summary: The murder of Henry Nowak has ignited a heated political debate in Britain. The police response to the case is central to the controversy. The article explores the events leading to the transatlantic discussion.

headline: King's nephew Peter Phillips weds NHS nurse with William and Kate among guests
summary: King Charles's nephew, Peter Phillips, married NHS nurse Harriet Sperling. The private ceremony was attended by Prince William and Kate. Phillips is the son of Princess Anne and 18th in line to the throne.

headline: Ukrainian drones target St Petersburg in attack Russia calls 'unprecedented'
summary: Ukrainian drones have targeted St Petersburg in an "unprecedented" attack. The city's governor urged residents to stay indoors for the first time in the war. This marks a significant escalation in drone activity targeting Russian territory.
